findings.evidence: `JHolomorphicLine.lean:139-154` proves only `ω (F stdComplexLineReal) (F stdComplexLineImag)`; `AlmostComplex.lean:187` gives `F (J₀ v) = J (F v)` for any complex-linear map, and `AlmostComplex.lean:324` defines tameness exactly as positivity of `ω w (J w)`.
findings.fix: Add the general `IsComplexLinearMap.symplecticForm_apply_apply_nonneg/pos` lemmas with variables `{J₀ : AlmostComplexStructure U} {F : U →ₗ[ℝ] V} (v : U)`, then derive the standard-line coordinate lemmas by applying them to `v := stdComplexLineReal`.
findings.issue: The nonnegativity/positivity area lemmas are special cases of the natural statement for any `IsComplexLinearMap J₀ J F` and any source vector `v`: `0 ≤ ω (F v) (F (J₀ v))`, and positive when `F v ≠ 0`.

summary: The derivative bookkeeping is naturally standard-line-specific, but the new symplectic area lemmas are proved only for the standard basis pair even though the same API should first exist for any complex-linear map and source vector.
